observation.summary: Release artifact docs were reader-authored while the workflow and packaging scripts already carried the executable facts.

hypothesis.summary: A release artifact manifest can generate the reader docs and verify workflow/script anchors for build targets, checksums, installer behavior, and archive boundaries.

execution.summary: Run releaseartifacts to check generated docs and upload evidence in public CI.

evaluation.summary: The verifier rejects reader doc drift and the architecture coverage tool no longer classifies release-artifacts as manual debt.

retrospective.summary: Release packaging knowledge becomes a generated projection of an executable manifest instead of a manually edited release note.

evidence.proves: release artifact docs are generated from the manifest and anchored to workflow/script source checks
public CI uploads release artifact docs evidence
